channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Biden heaps praise on Slovenia PM for aiding release of Americans in major US-Russia prisoner swap
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden heaped praise on Slovenian Prime Minister Robert Golob on Tuesday for his role in the recent seven-nation prisoner swap that freed Americans Evan Gershkovich, Alsu Kurmasheva and Paul Whelan from Russian prison. The deal, completed in August, was the largest U.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Waffles, pancakes recall expanded over potential listeria contamination
item.description: The FDA reported that the possible presence of listeria bacteria was detected through routine testing at the company's Brantford, Ontario, factory.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: North Carolina county surrounding Asheville overcounted Helene deaths by as many as 30, sheriff says
item.description: A sheriff’s office statement and data from the state show that the North Carolina county that is home to Asheville overcounted deaths caused by Helene by as many as 30.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge orders Rudy Giuliani to hand over assets to Georgia election workers
item.description: The order appoints the election workers as receivers of Rudy Giuliani’s New York apartment, Mercedes-Benz, luxury watches and some of his cash, among other items.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: 19-year-old woman found dead in Walmart oven
item.description: A Walmart store in eastern Canada was closed for a third day Tuesday as police investigated the death of a woman whose body was found inside an oven. Halifax Nova Scotia Regional Police said in a release that they were called at 9:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Jocelina Joiner, KTLA 5

item.title: 19-year-old woman found dead in oven in Canada Walmart
item.description: A Canadian Walmart store was closed for a third day Tuesday as police investigated the death of a woman whose body was found inside an oven.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump tells child US 'won’t have any cows' under Harris
item.description: The Harris-Walz team hit back by calling the claims a “delusional rant,” questioning the former president’s mental capacity.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Mike Jeffries, former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO, arrested on federal sex trafficking charges
item.description: Former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO Michael Jeffries and two other men have been arrested on sex trafficking and interstate prostitution charges, a spokesperson for federal prosecutors said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate Democrats running away from Harris in 'blue wall' states
item.description: Vulnerable Democratic Senate candidates in 'blue wall' states are distancing themselves from Vice President Harris.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Trump makes more debunked claims about FEMA as he visits storm damaged North Carolina
item.description: Trump was asked whether it was helpful to criticize hurricane relief workers after the Federal Emergency Management Agency recently paused work in the area because of reports they could be targeted by militia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Helicopter crashes into radio tower in Houston, killing 4
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Four people aboard a helicopter were killed when it crashed into a radio tower in Houston and burst into flames, officials said. The helicopter went down just before 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pinellas County man recovering after near-death experience with flesh-eating bacteria
item.description: A Pinellas County man is sharing his near death experience after contracting Vibrio Vulnificus, a flesh-eating bacteria. This comes as the Florida Department of Health is warning people to avoid floodwaters, as cases of the infection continue to rise after back-to-back hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Marino, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Migrants caught at border bussed, flown out of San Diego in possible 'cover up' before election: official
item.description: A San Diego County official criticized the flying of illegal immigrants out of California to other states so close to the presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs faces 7 new lawsuits, including alleged rape of 13-year-old
item.description: Seven new lawsuits have been filed against Sean ‘Diddy’ Combs, including one alleging the rape of a 13-year-old girl. They come as his lawyers tried again Monday to get him freed on bail, and complained that a “fresh wave of publicity” is endangering his right to a fair criminal trial.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Navy identifies 2 killed in fighter jet crash as aviators from California
item.description: The Navy identified on Monday the two crew members who died last week in a jet fighter crash near Mount Rainier as two 31-year-old aviators from California. Lt. Cmdr. Lyndsay P. Evans, a Naval Flight Officer, and Lt. Serena N.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Prosecutors: Ex-officer's gunshots put people in danger the night of deadly Breonna Taylor raid
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — The 10 shots Brett Hankison fired into Breonna Taylor 's apartment the night she was killed in a police raid put Taylor, her neighbors and even one of Hankison’s fellow Louisville police officers in danger, federal prosecutors argued Monday.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Paul Di'Anno, former Iron Maiden lead singer, dies at 66
item.description: Former Iron Maiden singer Paul Andrews, better known by his stage name Paul Di'Anno, has died at the age of 66.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, Fox 5

item.title: Tourists return to NYC's Vessel sculpture after it reopens with netting to prevent deaths
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Tourists once again climbed the steps of Manhattan's beehive-shaped Vessel sculpture after it reopened Monday for the first time in three years, now with netting aimed at decreasing the risk of suicides like the ones that forced its closure.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO and John Minchillo, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What is pink cocaine? Autopsy finds drug in Liam Payne's body
item.description: A partial autopsy conducted on former One Direction singer Liam Payne found multiple substances in his system when he fell to his death from the third-floor balcony of a hotel room in Argentina last week, according to a new report from ABC News.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, Fox 5

item.title: How the 2025 Social Security COLA compares to recent years
item.description: A recent survey from the Motley Fool shows 50% of retirees have considered going back to work to make ends meet.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, ABC 4

item.title: How does abortion translate? Ballot measures challenge interpreters
item.description: Vague or technical terms can be challenging, even more so when it comes to Indigenous languages that have only limited written dictionaries.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Oscar brings winds, heavy rains to eastern Cuba after striking the Bahamas
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Tropical Storm Oscar brought heavy rains and winds to Cuba, an island already beleaguered by a massive power outage, late Sunday after brushing the Bahamas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 Navy aviators declared dead after fighter jet crash in Washington state
item.description: The aviators' names won’t be released until a day after their next of kin have been notified, the Navy said in a statement Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Grandma who texted stranger for Thanksgiving announces breast cancer diagnosis
item.description: The Arizona grandmother who sent a text message to what she believed was her grandson’s phone number, inviting him over for Thanksgiving dinner nearly nine years ago, has announced her diagnosis of breast cancer.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Juan Soto's 3-run homer in 10th sends Yankees past Guardians 5-2 and into World Series for 41st time
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Juan Soto was supposed to be the difference-maker, the one who pushed the New York Yankees back to the top. They're one step away.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Learning about God from dogs, Orthodox monks breed and train canines in upstate New York monastery
item.description: CAMBRIDGE, New York (AP) — After he and his fellow monks sang morning prayers in their church nestled in a forest, Brother Luke walked back to his residence to be greeted by a different kind of choir.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Indiana 3-year-old scratched by monkey inside Walmart
item.description: BEDFORD, Ind. (WXIN) — A 3-year-old is recovering after being scratched this week by a monkey inside a central Indiana Walmart store. The incident reportedly occurred on Wednesday afternoon inside the Walmart Supercenter in Bedford, Indiana. Local police responded to the incident.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Child labor violations on the rise, problem could get worse: report
item.description: Between 2011 and 2020, the rate of injuries among workers under 18 nearly doubled, a recent report says.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, WGN 9

item.title: Why experts say keeping standard time is 'undeniably' better for us
item.description: Surveys have found that most Americans want to do away with the twice-a-year changing of the clocks. But whether we should observe daylight saving or standard time year round will depend on who you ask.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Indiana 3-year-old scratched by monkey inside Walmart
item.description: BEDFORD, Ind. (WXIN) — A 3-year-old is recovering after being scratched by a monkey inside a central Indiana Walmart store this week. The incident reportedly occurred on Wednesday afternoon inside the Walmart Supercenter in Bedford, Indiana. Local police responded to the incident.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, ABC 4

item.title: California woman slashed dog's throat, left it alive in trash: Sacramento deputies
item.description: The woman "felt her life would be easier if she only had to care for one dog and cut one of the dog’s throats," say officials.
item.dc:creator: Veronica Catlin, WGN 9

item.title: Walter White of 'Breaking Bad' returns for New Mexico litter campaign
item.description: Two television ads featuring Bryan Cranston as Walter White, directed by "Breaking Bad" creator Vince Gilligan, were created to promote clean-up of New Mexico areas.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Salinas, WGN 9

item.title: Trump compares imprisoned Jan. 6 rioters to Japanese Americans during WWII
item.description: Nearly 900 people have pleaded guilty to a variety of federal charges in relation to the events of Jan. 6.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Biden administration forgives student loans for 60,000 public service workers
item.description: The Biden administration announced Thursday that it is forgiving the student loans for more than 60,000 public service workers. The move gives $4.5 billion in debt relief to teachers, firefighters and other eligible workers who have been paying on their loans for more than 10 years.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9
